description_long: This is a tall, open shelving unit constructed primarily from wood, featuring a modular design with two vertical columns connected by a central horizontal shelf. The shelves are spaced at regular intervals, providing multiple storage surfaces, and the structure is supported by vertical wooden posts at each corner. Some of the shelves contain visible items, including a pile of white papers or documents and a few dark-colored objects, suggesting its use for both storage and display. The overall design is minimalist and functional, with no visible doors, handles, or enclosed compartments. The open sides and back make the contents easily accessible from multiple angles, and the natural wood finish gives it a warm, contemporary appearance. This type of shelving unit would be suitable for urban environments such as offices, libraries, or public spaces.
description: A tall, open wooden shelving unit with two vertical columns, multiple shelves, and visible stored items. Its minimalist, accessible design is ideal for storage and display in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing two vertical columns, multiple shelves, and visible contents including papers and dark objects.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the shelves and the open, accessible structure with no back panels.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with clear visibility of the shelves and stored items.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the open framework and the arrangement of shelves from a different angle.
